name: Oxono
author: Abstract Games factory (game by Tom Delahaye, Cosmoludo 2024)
description: Oxono (Tom Delahaye, Cosmoludo 2024): a double-natured four-in-a-row on a 6x6 board. Two SHARED totems - one X, one O - start on the central dot squares. On your turn, move a totem of your choice like a rook (over empty squares only), then place one of your tokens of the SAME symbol on an empty square orthogonally next to the totem's new position. A totem whose four neighbours are all occupied may instead JUMP over them to the first free square (and if its whole row and column are full, fly anywhere; if it lands fully enclosed, your token may go on any free square). First to line up 4 tokens of their COLOUR (any symbols) or 4 of one SYMBOL (any colours - the player completing the line wins it), horizontally or vertically, wins; totems never count in a line. If all 32 tokens are placed with no line, the game is a draw.
